common.welcome_description_2: Minimally functional SSO client with redirect, callback and local session flow.
common.welcome_description_3: The client is built on the `sso_server` authorize, token and userinfo foundation, while the protected pages remain behind the Laravel session + `auth` guard.
common.welcome_description: The central identity comes from the SSO server, but local roles and permissions remain in this client application.
common.auth_behavior.title: Auth Behavior
common.auth_behavior.description: Protected pages are redirected to the login page without a session, from where a single button starts the SSO redirect flow.
toolbar.bulk.none: There are no deletable records on this page.
toolbar.bulk.prompt: Select rows to enable bulk actions.
topbar.server.title: Central SSO control plane
topbar.client.title: Connected SSO workspace
navigation.dashboard.label: Dashboard
navigation.dashboard.description: Protected client pages are now accessible with working SSO redirection, callback, and session login.
navigation.profile.label: Profile
navigation.my_account.label: My Account
navigation.companies.label: Companies
navigation.companies.description: Complete administration of the local company database with search, filtering and authorization management.
navigation.employees.label: Employees
navigation.employees.description: Complete administration of the local employee directory with search, filtering, and authorization management.
navigation.users.label: Users
navigation.users.description: SSO projection based user list with readonly identity fields and client-specific local metadata.
navigation.roles.label: Roles
navigation.roles.description: Complete administration of local roles with permission assignments and local RBAC management.
navigation.permissions.label: Permissions
navigation.permissions.description: Complete administration of the local permission catalog for local role assignments.
navigation.connection_health.label: Connection Health
navigation.audit_logs.label: Audit Logs
sso_status.description: At a glance, this page shows whether the shared sign-in connection is ready, what it means for users, and what to do next.
sso_status.health_ready_description: Users can continue to the central sign-in flow without extra setup on this client.
sso_status.health_attention_description: This client still needs SSO setup or review before the connection can be trusted end to end.
sso_status.check_sign_in_route_ok: The client knows where to send users for sign-in.
sso_status.check_sign_in_route_missing: The client does not yet have a valid authorize endpoint.
sso_status.check_token_exchange_ok: The callback flow can exchange the authorization result for tokens.
sso_status.check_token_exchange_missing: The token endpoint is missing, so sign-in completion is at risk.
sso_status.check_callback_target_ok: The SSO server has a redirect target for this client flow.
sso_status.check_callback_target_missing: No redirect URI is configured for the SSO callback.
sso_status.check_local_fallback_ok: Local sign-in remains available if the central flow is temporarily unavailable.
sso_status.check_local_fallback_missing: This client relies fully on the central SSO path.
sso_status.open_my_account_description: Check the identity and role data this app currently exposes to you.
sso_status.review_profile_settings_descr: Use the self-service profile surface if you need to validate user-facing account details.
sso_status.next_step_ready_account: Use My Account to review what this app knows about your signed-in identity.
sso_status.next_step_ready_profile: Open Profile if you need to confirm self-service account details.
sso_status.next_step_ready_details: Only visit the technical details below when support or integration review needs it.
sso_status.next_step_setup_admin: Ask an administrator to verify the SSO server base URL and endpoints shown below.
sso_status.next_step_setup_fallback: Keep local access available until the redirect and token exchange are confirmed.
sso_status.next_step_setup_retry: Retry the sign-in flow only after the missing configuration items are fixed.
sso_status.technical_details_title: Technical details for support and integration review
sso_status.technical_details_description: These fields are still available for debugging, but they now sit behind a secondary disclosure so the page stays readable for normal users.
auth.login.title: Sign in
auth.login.description: The system redirects you to the central sign-in to continue.
profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Manage your account details and security settings.
profile.identity_description: Update your display name while the email remains the central identifier.
profile.password_description: Set a new strong password for your account.
profile.delete_description: This action is permanent. Save anything you still need before deleting the account.
profile.info_banner: This surface is for self-service only. Identity validation, persistence, password hashing, and audit logging still happen on the SSO server.
profile.api_unavailable: The upstream self-service profile API is not configured for this client environment.
profile.email_readonly_help: Email stays read-only in self-service so the shared client/server identity mapping remains stable.
profile.password_api_unavailable: Password change is unavailable until the upstream self-service API is configured.
profile.validation.password_min: New password must be at least 8 characters.
account.title: My Account
account.description: This page is the client-facing account summary layer that can later blend SSO identity data with local authorization state.
